title: Pungent Australian Pork Bao
description: A fermented and gooey Australian dish featuring pork buns and a sweet glaze, perfect for those who dislike onion and garlic.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped pork, fermented soybean paste, honey, rice vinegar, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Divide the bun dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the bun.
Place the buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with sesame oil.
Bake the buns for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the buns are baking, prepare the glaze by whisking together the honey and rice vinegar in a small bowl.
Remove the buns from the oven and brush with the glaze.
Return the buns to the oven and bake for an additional 2 minutes, or until the glaze is caramelized.
